items.content: Fix the login bug where users get a 401 after password reset
I'll investigate the login issue by reading the authentication handler first.

functionCalls.name: file_read

items.content: I see the issue — the login handler reads `user.passwordHash`, but after a password reset the column used is `password_hash_v2`. Let me check the test suite to confirm.

functionCalls.name: shell
functionCalls.output:
> webapp@2.1.0 test
> jest --grep login

 FAIL  src/auth/__tests__/login.test.ts
  handleLogin
    ✓ rejects invalid email (12 ms)
    ✓ rejects wrong password (8 ms)
    ✗ accepts valid credentials after password reset (15 ms)

  ● handleLogin › accepts valid credentials after password reset

    Expected: 200
    Received: 401

Test Suites: 1 failed, 1 total
Tests:       1 failed, 2 passed, 3 total

items.content: Confirmed — the test fails because the login handler doesn't check the updated password hash column. The fix is to read from `password_hash_v2` when it exists, falling back to `passwordHash` for legacy accounts.
